Home»Lifestyle»Celeb News»Royals»Princess Charlene dazzles at Paris 2024 Olympics opening

At the Paris 2024 Olympic opening ceremony, Prince Albert II, Princess Charlene, and their twins, made a striking appearance.

Prince Albert II of Monaco, Princess Charlene, and their twins, Princess Gabriella and Prince Jacques, enjoyed the Paris 2024 Olympic opening ceremony.

The royal family made a striking appearance, showcasing their impeccable style and deep-rooted connections to the Olympic Games.

Princess Charlene of Monaco is a former competitive swimmer who represented South Africa in the 2000 Sydney Olympics.

She graced the event in a custom Louis Vuitton white jumpsuit. Her choice reflected elegance and sophistication, according toWWD.

She paired it with metallic silver pumps and a chic Mini Capucines handbag from Vuitton.

Her husband, Prince Albert II, coordinated with the colours of Monaco’s flag. He sported a vibrant red blazer, white shirt, and matching trousers.

The twins, Princess Gabriella and Prince Jacques, also looked charming. This added a touch of youthful exuberance to the royal ensemble.

Prince Albert II, an accomplished athlete himself, competed in bobsleigh for Monaco in five consecutive Winter Olympics.

His deep connection to the Olympics extends beyond participation; he has been a member of the International Olympic Committee since 1985, according to theDaily Mail.

“The Olympics hold a special place in our hearts,” Prince Albert remarked. “We are thrilled to be here as a family to support the athletes.”

The royal couple attended a pre-Olympics reception for heads of state at the Elysee presidential palace.

They were welcomed by French President Emmanuel Macron and First Lady Brigitte Macron.

This reception set the stage for the spectacular opening ceremony that followed.

The ceremony, held along the river Seine, was a visual feast. Lady Gaga, Aya Nakamura, and Céline Dion delivered electrifying performances that enthralled the audience.

Serena Williams, U.S. First Lady Jill Biden, Chrissy Teigen, and Ariana Grande were among the notable attendees.

The ceremony featured the traditional parade of athletes from 206 national Olympic committees, travelling on nearly 100 boats along the Seine, according toGB News.

This unique procession passed iconic Parisian landmarks such as Notre Dame, the Louvre, and the Eiffel Tower. This added a touch of Parisian magic to the festivities.

Monaco’s presence at the Olympics was marked by five athletes.

Princess Charlene expressed herpride, stating, “It’s inspiring to see our athletes represent Monaco on this global stage.

Their dedication and hard work are truly commendable.”

The Paris 2024 Olympics, officially the Games of the XXXIII Olympiad, will run until 11 August.

This edition is particularly significant as it marks the centenary of the last time Paris hosted the Games in 1924.

The city has transformed itself into a vibrant hub of sporting excellence and cultural celebration.

As the Games continue, the presence of the Monaco royal family added a layer of prestige and glamour.

Their participation underscores the enduring bond between the monarchy and the Olympic movement.

With their athletic backgrounds and royal duties, Prince Albert II and Princess Charlene embody the spirit of the Olympics.

They celebrate unity, competition, and international camaraderie.
